things.name: Bray Promenade & Beach
things.description: Bray's Victorian promenade stretching along a shingle beach below the iconic Bray Head — the classic Irish seaside experience

things.name: Bray Head Cliff Walk to Greystones
things.description: One of Ireland's finest coastal cliff walks — 7km along the cliffs from Bray Head to the pretty harbour village of Greystones

things.name: Killruddery House & Gardens
things.description: 17th-century Brabazon estate with Ireland's oldest landscaped garden featuring a remarkable 17th-century twin canals and beech hedge theatre

things.name: Powerscourt Estate & Gardens (Day Trip)
things.description: Ireland's finest demesne — magnificent Italianate gardens, Powerscourt Waterfall (Ireland's highest), the Wicklow Mountains backdrop

things.name: National Sealife Centre Bray
things.description: Family-friendly aquarium on the seafront with sharks, rays and tropical fish — an entertaining introduction to ocean life

things.name: Wicklow Mountains National Park (Day Trip)
things.description: Ireland's largest national park — Glendalough's monastic ruins and two glacial lakes set in wild mountain scenery

things.name: Glendalough Monastic Site (Day Trip)
things.description: 6th-century monastic settlement founded by St Kevin — two mountain lakes, a round tower and a collection of early medieval churches

things.name: Ardmore Studios (Film Studios)
things.description: Ireland's national film studios where Braveheart, My Left Foot and many Hollywood productions were filmed — occasional open events

things.name: Avoca Handweavers (Day Trip)
things.description: The world's oldest surviving woollen mill in the Avoca valley — famous for distinctive colourful blankets, scarves and knitwear

things.name: DART Train to Dublin City (30 min)
things.description: Bray is the southern terminus of the DART coastal railway — 30 minutes brings you to Dublin city centre, Sandymount and Dalkey
